title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. tauricum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. tauricum, also known as bracken, is a subspecies of the fern genus Pteridium, specifically a type of evergreen fern native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. tauricum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of consuming Pteridium aquilinum subsp. tauricum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Traditionally, Pteridium aquilinum subsp. tauricum has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widespread due to potential toxicity concerns.
